scenarios.requirement: Record one clean exact qurl-go build SHA and the atomic public Hub host, UDP port, and server key.
Call RegisterAgentRuntime against the real public Hub and obtain a persisted registered runtime with an authenticated assigned-cell tuple.
Force Hub COK and prove the SDK's RKN reaches the public Hub and completes assignment.
Use an account credential to send exactly one assigned-cell OTP request before REG.
Prove duplicate/restarted account enrollment cannot dispatch a second OTP for one ticket.
Exercise the authenticated assigned-cell OTP rate-limit result and bounded client behavior.
Exercise terminal and retryable OTP failures without a hidden second send or HTTP fallback.
Call KnockRegisteredAgent with the Hub-assigned binding and receive the SDK's authenticated resource admission result.
Force assigned-cell COK and prove the SDK's RKN receives the authenticated ACK.
Call ExitRegisteredAgentSession with the exact public session identity and receive SDK success.
Refresh placement through the pinned Hub and record the resulting assigned-cell trust tuple.
Expire an ephemeral assignment and prove no cell traffic occurs until an authenticated Hub refresh succeeds.
Move one ephemeral agent from cell0 to real cell1 and contact only the newly assigned endpoint after explicit adoption.
Reject stale generation, revision, endpoint, or lease state before a datagram is sent.
Drop an authenticated RAK result and prove restart replays the exact persisted activation without a new assignment.
Restart at every durable registration boundary and resume the exact pending episode.
Revoke an ephemeral device credential and complete explicit Hub-to-cell UDP recovery with the same agent identity.
Complete, refresh, and recover across the real two-cell topology without cell inference or cross-cell fallback.
Change the Hub DNS answer and prove each exchange resolves the authoritative public name again.
Change the assigned-cell DNS answer and prove each exchange resolves the authenticated endpoint name again.
Fail closed on public Hub DNS failure with no HTTP, relay, inferred cell, or fallback endpoint.
Fail closed on assigned-cell DNS failure with no HTTP, relay, or cross-cell fallback.
Reject a Hub response that cannot authenticate to the configured Hub public key.
Reject a cell response that cannot authenticate to the Hub-assigned cell public key.
Inject an otherwise valid response from the wrong network source and prove the client/server source binding fails closed.
Use the wrong authenticated peer/caller against Hub and cell operations and observe terminal denial.
Replay captured request and response datagrams and observe authenticated replay rejection without state advancement.
Duplicate datagrams client-side and prove idempotent or terminal behavior matches the released contract.
Drop selected datagrams and prove bounded retry or durable recovery with no new logical operation.
Reorder datagrams client-side and prove stale/out-of-order packets cannot advance lifecycle state.
Delay selected datagrams across retry and lease boundaries and observe bounded contract behavior.
Force UDP timeouts and observe the exact retry/recovery taxonomy without HTTP fallback.
Cancel at each network phase and prove no post-boundary datagram or unsafe state mutation occurs.
Inject authenticated malformed envelopes and application bodies and observe generic fail-closed errors.
Inject over-limit request/reply material and prove the 4096-byte packet boundary fails closed.
Inject an authenticated unknown or phase-invalid NHP message and observe terminal rejection.
Open the just-persisted native runtime without an enrollment credential or network enrollment call.
Register from a real sandbox KMS-backed sealed store without leaving plaintext durable key state.
Restart from sealed state and knock successfully without any enrollment or recovery credential.
Observe zero calls through both the injected SDK HTTP client and process-wide default HTTP transport.
Attribute the exact Hub LST/LRT, assigned-cell REG/RAK, and completion LST/LRT packet sequence to the ephemeral agent.
Attribute the exact assigned-cell KNK/ACK and EXT/ACK sequence, including any forced COK/RKN, to the ephemeral session.
Run the exact Connector candidate in its hardened Linux container on the dedicated EC2 proof runner.
Prove native KNK admission and authenticated FRP login complete before any proxy registration starts.
Carry real application traffic through the admitted FRP path and verify the expected backend response.
Prove canonical public resource_id remains distinct from placement knock_resource_id through provisioning and knock.
Crash at each provision boundary and reconcile without duplicate or lost resource identity.
Crash at each removal boundary and retain the journal until authoritative deletion is proven.
Restart the container from sealed state after physically removing the enrollment-secret mount source.
Capture the lifecycle and prove no bootstrap, assignment, OTP, REG, completion, relay, refresh, or recovery HTTP route is contacted.
Bind qurl-go, Connector, NHP, qurl-service, qRTS, FRP commits and image digests into one redacted evidence manifest.
Record DNS, server key, destination, source, identity-tuple hash, message outcome, and API-route-count observations.
Provide a live public Hub, dark Authority, cell0, cell1, LayerV DNS/trust roots, OTP/SES, and safe ephemeral mutation controls.
Provide the reviewed-source-CIDR EC2 runner with Docker, NET_ADMIN, packet capture, narrow KMS role, UDP proxy, and tc netem.
